issues.claim: No claim that the Gita is really a tech manual
issues.objection: 7.21 explicitly preserves the verse as a verse: literal_meaning, traditional_meaning_consensus, verbatim Shankara/Ramanuja under fair-use, source pack with full sectarian attribution. Engineering layer is presented as operational reflection at narrower scope, with STRETCHED tagging where appropriate. Translation field opens by acknowledging the metaphysical reach (where applicable) before describing the operational analog. The verse is not flattened into a productivity nugget. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Verse claims a personal divine agency (vidadhāmy aham — I make firm); engineering analog stays at structural-pattern scope. Counter-example explicitly carves out structurally-inappropriate technique-fit so the analog does not collapse into 'commit to anything.'

notes: Tech-manual framing check for 7.21: the engineering layer is additive, not substitutive. PASS.
